html:
[807]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 判官密言赦文不明白 - A phrase indicating that the judge or official secretly says that the decree of clemency is unclear or ambiguous.
  • 再堕其四 - A cryptic or possibly metaphorical phrase. '再堕' may mean 'to fall again' or 'to fall twice,' while '其四' could refer to the fourth of something. This may be a numerological or symbolic expression.
  • 只赦两人 - 'Only two are forgiven' or 'only two are pardoned.'
  • 其一则赵氏也 - 'One of them is the Zhao family' or 'One of the two is from the Zhao clan.'
  • 无碍无遮广聪明 - A magical or incantational phrase, possibly a mantra or spell meant to enhance wisdom or intelligence.
  • 矞律莎诃无紧揭 - Another incantational phrase, possibly of foreign or esoteric origin, with possible phonetic or transliterated elements.
  • 聪明偈云 - 'A verse or hymn concerning wisdom' or 'A chant for wisdom.'
  • 大广天地无碍遮 - A poetic or metaphysical expression, likely from a verse or mantra.
  • 一界迟奇比江海 - A metaphorical or symbolic phrase, possibly comparing the vastness of the mind or universe to the sea.
  • 一磨二磨转不觉 - A phrase suggesting that through repeated effort (grinding), one becomes unaware of the process, or that the process is gradual and imperceptible.
  • 才管一觉无碍空 - A phrase possibly referring to the moment of awakening or realization, where there is no obstruction or limitation.
  • 戒令勿泄 - 'Warned not to reveal' or 'Instructed not to disclose.'
  • 每遇节序 - 'Every time a festival or seasonal event occurs.'
  • 焚香默诵百过 - 'Burn incense and silently recite it a hundred times.'
  • 人心如镜 - A common metaphor meaning 'the human mind is like a mirror.'
  • 须管常磨 - 'Must be constantly polished' or 'Must be constantly maintained.'
  • 勿令尘染污 - 'Do not let it become stained by dust,' a metaphor for preserving purity of mind.
  • 自然聪明 - 'Naturally intelligent' or 'Becomes wise naturally.'
  • 吾一身五职 - 'My one body has five roles' or 'I have five duties in one body.'
  • 第一三天门下引进主者 - A title or position, possibly referring to a role in the celestial or bureaucratic hierarchy.
  • 第二黄先生主掌文字 - 'Second, Mr. Huang is in charge of texts' or 'Second, Mr. Huang oversees written matters.'
  • 第三自然山主 - 'Third, the master of Natural Mountain' or 'Third, the ruler of Natural Mountain.'
  • 第四监灰河主 - 'Fourth, the overseer of the Ash River' or 'Fourth, the master of the Ash River.'
  • 遂引叔介至灰河 - 'Then he led Shu Jie to the Ash River.'
  • 无罪者过桥 - 'The innocent cross the bridge.'
  • 业重者解其下服 - 'Those with heavy karma are stripped of their lower garments' or 'Those with heavy karmic burden are stripped of their lower attire.'

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 判官 - A title for an official or judicial figure, possibly a judge or a supernatural judge in a bureaucratic or Daoist/Confucian framework.
  • 赦文 - A decree of clemency or pardon, often issued by a ruler or in a supernatural context.
  • 赵氏 - The Zhao family, a common surname in Chinese history, possibly referring to a specific person or lineage.
  • 无碍无遮广聪明 - Likely a mantra or incantation from a Buddhist or Daoist tradition meant to enhance wisdom or remove mental obstructions.
  • 矞律莎诃无紧揭 - Possibly a transliteration of a foreign or esoteric term, possibly from Sanskrit or another language, used in a magical or ritual context.
  • 聪明偈 - A 'verse of wisdom' or 'hymn for wisdom,' possibly from a Buddhist or Daoist text.
  • 人心如镜 - A common metaphor in Chinese philosophy, especially in Buddhism and Daoism, comparing the mind to a mirror that should be kept clean.
  • 灰河 - The 'Ash River,' possibly a symbolic or supernatural river associated with judgment or karma.
  • 无罪者过桥 - 'The innocent cross the bridge' – a metaphor for passing judgment or a test of innocence.
  • 业重者解其下服 - 'Those with heavy karma are stripped of their lower garments' – a metaphor for the punishment or judgment of those with grave sins or karmic burdens.

Proper Nouns

  • 判官 - A title for a judge or official, possibly in a supernatural or bureaucratic context.
  • 赵氏 - The Zhao family, a common surname in Chinese history.
  • 黄先生 - 'Mr. Huang,' a title or name, possibly a scholar or official.
  • 自然山 - 'Natural Mountain,' a symbolic or actual place name.
  • 灰河 - 'Ash River,' likely a symbolic or supernatural location.
  • 叔介 - 'Shu Jie,' likely a person's name or title, possibly a student or disciple.

[808]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 着度河裈 - A literal description of a person wearing a specific type of garment ('裈' is a type of undergarment) while crossing a river.
  • 岸上大枯木数株 - A literal description of large, dead trees on the riverbank.
  • 鬼卒以所脱衣挂于上 - A description of ghost soldiers hanging clothes on the trees, likely a metaphorical or supernatural element.
  • 续以车载从桥行 - A description of a vehicle following the bridge, possibly a cart or carriage.
  • 衣上各书姓名 - A literal description of names written on the clothes.
  • 窥其一标云 - A phrase indicating the act of looking at one of the labels, which reads '屠氏十娘'.
  • 叔介临欲归 - A phrase indicating that 'Shu Jie' is about to return home.
  • 拜将军曰 - A formal act of bowing to a general and speaking to him.
  • 自到冥间 - A phrase indicating that the person has arrived in the underworld.
  • 荷将军慈顾 - A phrase expressing gratitude for the general's kindness.
  • 忆昔尝与汝同官 - A phrase recalling a past shared experience, likely in the same office or position.
  • 曾缘公累 - A phrase indicating that the person was once in a difficult situation due to their position.
  • 赖汝调护得免 - A phrase expressing gratitude for being helped out of a difficult situation.
  • 至今不忘 - A phrase indicating that the memory of this help is still vivid.
  • 今归时凡此中所见所说 - A phrase indicating that the person will recount everything they have seen and heard in the underworld.
  • 尽为人道之 - A phrase indicating that the person will share this information with others to serve as a warning.
  • 使知省戒 - A phrase indicating the purpose of sharing the information is to make others aware and cautious.
  • 无得隐情 - A phrase indicating that nothing should be hidden.
  • 揖别而行 - A phrase indicating a formal farewell and departure.
  • 望其家已近 - A phrase indicating that the person's home is in sight.
  • 母在一室涂泽毕 - A phrase indicating that the mother is in a room, having completed some preparations (possibly for a ritual or ceremony).
  • 令引至坛 - A phrase indicating that someone is leading the person to a platform or altar.
  • 对曰 - A phrase indicating a response or reply.
  • 黄先生不许孝子登坛 - A phrase indicating that 'Mr. Huang' does not allow the person to ascend the platform.
  • 母乃独登之 - A phrase indicating that the mother ascends the platform alone.
  • 遍礼列位 - A phrase indicating that the mother performs a ritual of respect to all the positions or individuals on the platform.
  • 诣黄君幙前 - A phrase indicating that the mother approaches the tent or enclosure of 'Mr. Huang'.
  • 焚香拜曰 - A phrase indicating that the mother burns incense and bows, saying something.
  • 谢救苦 - A phrase indicating that the mother thanks 'Mr. Huang' for saving her from suffering.
  • 黄法师便冉冉翔空 - A phrase indicating that 'Mr. Huang' (a Taoist priest) ascends into the sky in a graceful manner.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 冥间 - Refers to the underworld or afterlife in Chinese belief.
  • 鬼卒 - Refers to ghost soldiers or spirits in the underworld, often associated with guiding or punishing the dead.
  • 黄先生 - Likely refers to a Taoist priest or spiritual leader, given the context of rituals and ascension.
  • 孝子 - Refers to a filial son, someone who is deeply devoted to their parents.
  • 黄君幙前 - Refers to the tent or enclosure of 'Mr. Huang,' likely a place of spiritual or ritual significance.
  • 救苦 - A Taoist concept referring to the act of saving someone from suffering, often associated with deities or spiritual leaders.

Proper Nouns

  • 叔介 - Likely a personal name, possibly referring to a historical or fictional figure.
  • 黄先生 - Likely a title or name for a Taoist priest or spiritual leader.
  • 黄君幙前 - Likely a place name or reference to a specific location in the ritual setting.

[809]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 回首言宿世冤家。皆得解脱。汝勿复悲恼 - Set phrases conveying a message of past grievances being resolved and a call not to grieve or be distressed.
  • 令从者取盂水噀叔界面。仍叱之 - Instructions for a servant to take water from a bowl and sprinkle it on the face of a relative while shouting at them.
  • 遂寤 - Means 'awoke' or 'woke up.'
  • 天方明 - Indicates that it was just getting light.
  • 自寝至觉仅数刻 - Describes the short duration from sleeping to waking, where '数刻' refers to a few moments or hours.
  • 而所经历闻见。连日言之不能尽 - Suggests that the experiences and things seen or heard were so numerous that they could not be recounted over several days.
  • 魏公以其事物色之 - Means that Hui Gong observed the events with a certain attitude or emotion.
  • 盖醮筵置龙虎堂。于四厢逼近外庖 - Describes the setup of a banquet hall with a dragon and tiger hall placed near the kitchen area.
  • 往来喧杂 - Indicates that there was a lot of noise and activity.
  • 炷香者乃老卒 - Refers to the person lighting incense as an old soldier.
  • 而汲水一兵患疥痨 - Describes a soldier who was suffering from scabies.
  • 圃中儿每敖戏圣位前 - Refers to children in the garden playing in front of a sacred place.
  • 皆符其语 - Means that everything matched what was said.
  • 乃告白龙虎神。徙位于静处。而易执事者 - Describes the act of informing the dragon and tiger gods and moving their positions to a quieter place, as well as changing the people responsible for certain duties.
  • 禁儿勿得至 - Means to forbid the children from coming.
  • 又考所谓潭字之误 - Indicates a review of the error in the word '潭.'
  • 盖词文旧语内云。或死于水涛之中 - Refers to a phrase in the old text that mentions dying in the waves.
  • 道童书涛为淘。以唾润指 - Describes a Taoist child writing '涛' as '淘' and moistening his fingers with saliva.
  • 揩作涛字。不甚明了 - Means that the character was written but not very clearly.
  • 故读者误焉 - Indicates that readers misunderstood due to the unclear writing.
  • 魏公自作记五千言 - Means that Hui Gong wrote a record of 5,000 words.
  • 今摭取其大要如此 - Suggests that this is a summary of the main points.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 魏公 - Refers to a person of high rank, possibly a noble or official.
  • 严州 - An ancient name for a city, now known as Yiwu in Zhejiang Province.
  • 宣州 - Another ancient name for a city, now known as Xuancheng in Anhui Province.
  • 平江 - An ancient name for a city, now known as Suzhou in Jiangsu Province.
  • 昆山 - A place name, now a district in Suzhou.
  • 绍兴二十八年 - The twenty-eighth year of the Shaoxing era, which corresponds to 1158 AD.
  • 洪内翰 - Refers to Hong Neihuan, a high-ranking official in the imperial court.
  • 翰林学士 - Refers to a scholar in the Hanlin Academy, a prestigious institution in the imperial court.

[810]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 常熟圬者 - 常熟 is the name of a place (Changshu, a county in Jiangsu Province), and 圬者 refers to a mason or wall-builder.
  • 中大夫吴温彦 - 中大夫 is an ancient Chinese official title, and 吴温彦 is the name of a person.
  • 累为郡守 - 累 means 'repeatedly' or 'successively,' and 郡守 refers to a prefect or governor.
  • 建第方成 - 建第 means 'to build a mansion,' and 方成 means 'just completed.'
  • 每夕必梦七人衣白衣 - 每夕 means 'every night,' 必 means 'necessarily,' 梦 means 'dream,' 七人 means 'seven people,' and 衣白衣 means 'wearing white clothes.'
  • 以告家人 - 以告 means 'to inform,' and 家人 means 'family members.'
  • 莫晓何祥也 - 莫晓 means 'no one understands,' and 何祥 means 'what omen.'
  • 未几得疾不起 - 未几 means 'soon after,' 得疾 means 'fell ill,' and 不起 means 'did not recover.'
  • 命役夫升屋 - 命 means 'ordered,' 役夫 means 'laborers,' and 升屋 means 'to climb the roof.'
  • 撤瓦遍观 - 撤瓦 means 'remove tiles,' and 遍观 means 'inspect thoroughly.'
  • 得纸人七枚于其中 - 得 means 'found,' 纸人 means 'paper figures,' 七枚 means 'seven pieces,' and 于其中 means 'in it.'
  • 乃圬者以佣直不满志 - 乃 means 'it turned out to be,' 圬者 refers to the mason, 以 means 'because,' 佣直 means 'wages,' and 不满志 means 'dissatisfied.'
  • 故为厌胜之术 - 故 means 'therefore,' 为 means 'to perform,' 厌胜之术 means 'a curse or jinx.'
  • 以祸主人 - 以 means 'to,' 祸 means 'harm,' and 主人 means 'the owner.'
  • 时王显道奂为郡守 - 时 means 'at that time,' 王显道奂 is the name of a person, and 为郡守 means 'served as prefect.'
  • 闻之 - 闻 means 'heard,' and 之 means 'it.'
  • 尽捕群匠送狱 - 尽捕 means 'arrested all,' 群匠 means 'all the craftsmen,' and 送狱 means 'sent to prison.'
  • 皆杖脊配远州 - 皆 means 'all,' 杖脊 means 'flogged on the back,' and 配远州 means 'exiled to a distant state.'
  • 吴人之俗 - 吴人 refers to people from Wu (a region in Jiangsu), and 之俗 means 'customs.'
  • 每覆瓦时 - 每 means 'every,' 覆瓦 means 'laying tiles,' and 时 means 'time.'
  • 虽盛暑 - 虽 means 'even if,' and 盛暑 means 'hot summer.'
  • 亦遣子弟亲登其上临视 - 亦 means 'also,' 遣 means 'send,' 子弟 means 'children or descendants,' 亲登其上 means 'climb up personally,' and 临视 means 'inspect.'
  • 盖惧此也 - 盖 means 'because,' 惧 means 'fear,' and 此 means 'this.'
  • 吴君北入不知此 - 吴君 refers to the protagonist, 北入 means 'went north,' and 不知此 means 'did not know this.'
  • 故堕其邪计 - 故 means 'therefore,' 堕 means 'failed,' and 邪计 means 'evil plan.'
  • 干道五年六月 - 干道 is a reign title, 五年 means 'fifth year,' and 六月 means 'June.'
  • 平江茶肆民家 - 平江 is the name of a place (Pingjiang, another name for Suzhou), 茶肆 means 'tea shop,' and 民家 means 'common people's home.'
  • 失其十岁儿 - 失 means 'lost,' 其 means 'his,' and 十岁儿 means 'ten-year-old child.'
  • 父母连日出求访 - 父母 means 'parents,' 连日 means 'for several days,' and 出求访 means 'went out to search.'
  • 但留幼女守舍 - 但 means 'only,' 留 means 'left,' 幼女 means 'young daughter,' and 守舍 means 'to guard the house.'
  • 一黄衣卒来啜茶 - 一 means 'one,' 黄衣 means 'yellow robe,' 卒 means 'soldier,' and 来啜茶 means 'came to drink tea.'
  • 告云 - 告 means 'told,' and 云 means 'that.'
  • 尔家几郎使我寄语 - 尔家 means 'your family,' 几郎 means 'young master,' and 使我寄语 means 'asked me to deliver a message.'
  • 早晚当附木筏还家 - 早晚 means 'soon,' 当 means 'will,' 附 means 'attach,' 木筏 means 'wooden raft,' and 还家 means 'return home.'
  • 女喜祈客少驻 - 女 means 'the daughter,' 喜 means 'happy,' 祈 means 'plead,' 客 means 'guest,' and 少驻 means 'stay a little longer.'
  • 以俟父母归 - 以 means 'in order to,' 俟 means 'wait,' 父母 means 'parents,' and 归 means 'return.'
  • 坚不可 - 坚 means 'firmly,' and 不可 means 'cannot.'
  • 临去又云 - 临去 means 'as he was leaving,' and 又云 means 'and said.'
  • 明日几郎自别寄信来 - 明日 means 'tomorrow,' 几郎 means 'young master,' 自别 means 'from another place,' and 寄信来 means 'will send a letter.'
  • 遂去 - 遂 means 'then,' and 去 means 'left.'
  • 迨暮 - 迨 means 'until,' and 暮 means 'evening.'

Proper Nouns

  • 干道 - A reign title.
  • 平江 - Another name for Suzhou.
  • 王显道奂 - The name of a person who served as prefect.

[811]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 樂橋妖 - Compound noun referring to a supernatural occurrence at the bridge of Le, likely a local legend or folk tale.
  • 既嫁 - Literally 'already married,' used here to introduce the narrative context.
  • 每夕為妖物所擾 - Literally 'every night troubled by a demonic entity,' indicating the recurring nature of the disturbance.
  • 母念之切 - Literally 'the mother's concern was intense,' emphasizing the mother's deep worry.
  • 乃與同榻卧 - Literally 'then slept in the same bed with her,' describing the mother's protective action.
  • 將伺察之 - Literally 'to spy on it,' indicating the mother's intention to observe the situation.
  • 財日暮 - Literally 'as night fell,' setting the time frame for the events.
  • 一人從地踊起 - Literally 'one person leaped up from the ground,' describing the sudden appearance of the entity.
  • 垂兩髻於背 - Literally 'with two braids hanging from the back,' a vivid description of the entity's appearance.
  • 紅繻弈然 - Literally 'red silk shining brightly,' describing the entity's attire.
  • 大声如疾雷 - Literally 'a loud sound like a thunderclap,' emphasizing the noise.
  • 地亦随合 - Literally 'the ground also closed,' indicating the entity's disappearance.
  • 凡数夕如是 - Literally 'for several nights it was like this,' summarizing the recurring events.
  • 以告其夫 - Literally 'to tell her husband,' indicating the mother's decision to inform the husband.
  • 夫穿地觅之 - Literally 'the husband dug into the ground to find it,' describing the husband's action.
  • 仅二尺许 - Literally 'only about two feet deep,' providing a specific measurement.
  • 得一铜铃 - Literally 'found a copper bell,' describing the object discovered.
  • 以红带系其鼻 - Literally 'with a red ribbon tied to its nose,' describing the bell's attachment.
  • 始忆数年前朝廷申严铜禁 - Literally 'only then did they remember that several years ago the court had strictly enforced copper restrictions,' explaining the background.
  • 故瘗铃土中 - Literally 'so they buried the bell in the ground,' describing the action taken.
  • 久而忘之矣 - Literally 'and forgot it after a long time,' indicating the lapse in memory.
  • 即击碎弃之 - Literally 'they immediately broke it and threw it away,' describing the resolution.
  • 女疾遂愈 - Literally 'the girl's illness then healed,' indicating the outcome.
  • 右四事皆朱似叔召说 - Literally 'the above four events were all told by Zhu Sishu,' attributing the story to a specific source.
  • 承议郎任随成 - A title and name, likely a historical figure.
  • 刘景文季孙甥也 - Literally 'Liu Jingwen was the nephew of Ji Sun,' providing a familial relationship.
  • 言景文知忻州时 - Literally 'Jingwen said when he was governor of Xinzhou,' setting the context.
  • 每数日辄一谒晋文公祠 - Literally 'every few days he would visit the shrine of Duke Wen of Jin,' describing the regular visits.
  • 至必与神偶语 - Literally 'when he arrived, he would converse with the deity,' indicating the nature of the interactions.
  • 移时乃出 - Literally 'after some time, he would leave,' describing the duration.
  • 神亦时时入郡 - Literally 'the deity also occasionally entered the city,' indicating the deity's presence.
  • 郡吏见景文闭合与客语 - Literally 'the city officials saw Jingwen shut the door and talk with guests,' describing the officials' observations.
  • 则神至也 - Literally 'then the deity had come,' indicating the connection.
  • 他日于广坐中 - Literally 'one day in a large gathering,' setting the scene.
  • 谓一曹掾曰 - Literally 'he told one of the clerks,' indicating the speaker and audience.
  • 天帝当来召君 - Literally 'the Heavenly Emperor will come to summon you,' predicting a divine call.
  • 君即去 - Literally 'you will then leave,' indicating the clerk's departure.
  • 吾且继往 - Literally 'I will follow shortly,' indicating Jingwen's intention.
  • 坐客相视失色 - Literally 'the guests looked at each other in alarm,' describing their reaction.
  • 未几 - Literally 'not long after,' indicating a short period.
  • 掾果无疾而逝 - Literally 'the clerk indeed passed away without illness,' describing the clerk's death.
  • 景文亦相继亡 - Literally 'Jingwen also died shortly after,' indicating Jingwen's subsequent death.
  • 经夕 - Literally 'after a night,' setting the time frame.
  • 蹶然复苏 - Literally 'he suddenly revived,' describing Jingwen's revival.
  • 索笔作三诗 - Literally 'he asked for a brush to write three poems,' describing the action.
  • 诗成语家人曰 - Literally 'the poems were spoken to his family,' indicating the content.
  • 吾今掌事雷部中 - Literally 'I now hold office in the Thunder Department,' indicating Jingwen's new role.
  • 不复为世间人矣 - Literally 'I am no longer a person of the world,' indicating his transformation.
  • 瞑目竟死 - Literally 'he closed his eyes and died,' describing his final moments.
  • 其一章云 - Literally 'one of the chapters says,' introducing the poem.
  • 中宫在天半 - Literally 'the central palace is in the middle of the sky,' a poetic description.
  • 其上乃吾家 - Literally 'above it is my home,' indicating a personal connection.
  • 纷纷鸾凤舞 - Literally 'various phoenixes dance,' a vivid image.
  • 往往芝术华 - Literally 'often the magic grass blooms,' another poetic image.
  • 挥手谢世人 - Literally 'waving goodbye to the world,' indicating his departure.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 樂橋 - A specific location, likely a bridge in a town or village, where the story takes place.
  • 晋文公祠 - The shrine of Duke Wen of Jin, a historical figure from the Spring and Autumn period.
  • 承议郎 - A title of a mid-level official in the Tang and Song dynasties.
  • 忻州 - A prefecture in Shanxi province, China.
  • 雷部 - The department of thunder in Chinese mythology, associated with the god of thunder and lightning.

Proper Nouns

  • 承议郎任随成 - A title and name, likely a historical figure.
  • 刘景文季孙甥也 - Literally 'Liu Jingwen was the nephew of Ji Sun,' providing a familial relationship.
  • 晋文公祠 - The shrine of Duke Wen of Jin, a historical figure from the Spring and Autumn period.
  • 忻州 - A prefecture in Shanxi province, China.

[812]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 竦身入云霞 - Poetic expression meaning 'to leap into the clouds and mist,' often used to describe an ascent into the heavens or a transcendent experience.
  • 公暇咏天海 - Literally 'to compose poetry about the sky and sea during leisure,' suggesting a contemplative or philosophical engagement with vast, natural themes.
  • 我非世人哗 - Means 'I am not part of the clamor of the world,' indicating a detachment from worldly concerns or a preference for solitude.
  • 仙都非世间 - Means 'the fairy realm is not of this world,' emphasizing the otherworldly nature of the realm being described.
  • 天神绕楼殿 - Means 'heavenly gods surround the halls and palaces,' suggesting a divine or sacred atmosphere.
  • 高低霞雾匀 - Means 'the mist and clouds are evenly distributed at all levels,' describing a harmonious and balanced natural environment.
  • 左右虬龙遍 - Means 'coiling dragons are everywhere to the left and right,' symbolizing power and protection.
  • 云车山岳耸 - Means 'cloud-capped mountains rise up,' depicting a majestic and elevated landscape.
  • 风颦天地擅 - Means 'the wind makes the earth and sky tremble,' suggesting a powerful and dynamic natural force.
  • 从兹得旧渥 - Means 'from here, one receives old favors,' implying a sense of continuity or inherited blessings.
  • 万动毫端变 - Means 'ten thousand things change at the tip of the brush,' suggesting the transformative power of writing or art.
  • 从来英杰自消磨 - Means 'from ancient times, heroes have been worn down,' indicating the inevitable decline of even the greatest figures.
  • 好笑人□事更多 - Means 'how many more things are laughable in the world,' suggesting a sense of irony or skepticism about human affairs.

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 洪州通判 - Title referring to a local administrative officer in the state of Hongzhou; often translated as 'vice-governor' or 'assistant governor.'
  • 鄉人李賓王 - Refers to a local person named Li Binwang; '鄉人' (xiangren) means 'a person from the same village or locality.'
  • 利用 - Character name, transliterated as 'Li Yongli.'
  • 知新淦縣 - Means 'served as the magistrate of Xingan County'; '知縣' (zhixian) is the title for a county magistrate.
  • 宣抚使 - High-ranking official responsible for military and civil affairs in a region; '宣抚' (xuanfu) means 'pacify and console.'
  • 躬至村墟督賦 - Means 'personally went to the villages to supervise the collection of taxes'; '躬至' (gongzhi) means 'to go personally,' '村墟' (cunxu) means 'villages,' and '督賦' (dufu) means 'supervise the collection of taxes.'
  • 劍檛傳呼曰 - Means 'with a sword beating and shouting'; '劍檛' (jianzha) refers to a sword beating, and '傳呼' (chuahu) means 'to shout or call out.'
  • 孤寒如是 - Means 'so poor and destitute'; '孤寒' (gohan) is a term describing someone who is poor and lonely.
  • 方大軍絡繹過縣 - Means 'when large armies were passing through the county'; '絡繹' (luoyi) means 'in succession' or 'continuously.'
  • 幸不以乏興為罪 - Means 'fortunately, they did not consider it a fault for being poor'; '乏興' (faxing) means 'lack of resources' or 'poor,' and '為罪' (weizui) means 'to consider as a crime.'
  • 得供給糧餉足矣 - Means 'to get enough supplies of food and grain is sufficient'; '供給' (gongji) means 'to provide,' '糧餉' (liangxiang) means 'food and grain,' and '足矣' (zuyi) means 'is sufficient.'
  • 別乘非所望也 - Means 'having a high-ranking official visit was not what was expected'; '別乘' (biercheng) refers to a high-ranking official, and '非所望也' (feisuoye) means 'not what was hoped for.'
  • 滿秩 - Means 'the end of the term of office'; '滿秩' (manzhi) refers to the completion of a term as an official.
  • 果為洪州倅 - Means 'indeed became the vice-governor of Hongzhou'; '倅' (cui) is a title for a deputy official, often translated as 'vice-governor.'
  • 卒如仆夢 - Means 'finally, as the servant had dreamed'; '卒' (zu) means 'finally' or 'in the end,' and '如仆夢' (rupeng) means 'as the servant had dreamed.'

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 洪州 - Refers to Hongzhou, an ancient name for Nanchang, the capital of Jiangxi Province in modern China.
  • 新淦縣 - Refers to Xingan County, a county in Jiangxi Province during the Song Dynasty, part of the Jiangxi circuit.
  • 宣抚使 - High-ranking official during the Song Dynasty responsible for both military and civil affairs in a region; often given to officials tasked with maintaining order and stability in troubled areas.
  • 通判 - Local administrative officer appointed by the central government to oversee the affairs of a county or prefecture; acted as a check on the power of the county magistrate and ensured loyalty to the central government.
  • 绍兴二年 - Refers to the second year of the Shaoxing era (1132 CE) during the Southern Song Dynasty; '绍兴' (Shaoxing) was the reign title of Emperor Gaozong (Zhao Gou).
  • 仆梦 - Refers to a servant's dream, a common motif in Chinese literature where dreams often foreshadow future events.

Proper Nouns

  • 洪州 - Translated as 'Hongzhou' or 'Nanchang.'
  • 新淦縣 - Translated as 'Xingan County.'
  • 李賓王 - Translated as 'Li Binwang.'
  • 利用 - Translated as 'Li Yongli.'
  • 绍兴二年 - Translated as 'the second year of the Shaoxing era (1132 CE).'

  • 洪州通判 - Translated as 'Vice-Governor of Hongzhou' or 'Tongpan of Hongzhou.'

  • 徽宗命建樓以臨之 - 'Emperor Huizong ordered the construction of a pavilion to overlook it,' indicating the emperor’s desire to have a vantage point over the garden.

  • 夢金紫人言曰 - 'Dreamed of a man in gold and purple robes who said,' referencing a dream vision and the colors of official rank (gold and purple signified high rank).
  • 取唐杜甫詩所謂天寒翠袖薄日暮倚修竹之句也 - 'Taking the line from the Tang poet Du Fu’s poem that says, 'On a cold day, the green sleeves are thin, at dusk she leans on tall bamboo.' This is a direct quotation from a classical poem used to justify the name.
  • 夢中問汝何人 - 'In the dream, I asked who you are,' a standard narrative device.
  • 對曰、臣乃太平宰相 - 'He replied, 'I am the Prime Minister of Peace.' This is likely symbolic or allegorical, as there was no historical figure known by this title during the time of Emperor Huizong.
  • 寤而異之 - 'Awakening and finding it strange,' a phrase used to indicate wonder or confusion after a dream.
  • 明旦、翰林學士李邦彦入對 - 'The next day, the Hanlin scholar Li Bangyan entered the court,' indicating a formal court appearance.

  • 偶問曰 - 'Incidentally asked,' indicating a casual or spontaneous question.
  • 近于苑中立小楼 - 'Recently, a small pavilion was built in the garden,' a past-tense reference to the pavilion's construction.
  • 下有修竹 - 'Below it are tall bamboo plants,' a descriptive phrase.
  • 當以何為名 - 'What should it be named?' a question about naming.
  • 邦彦了不经思 - 'Li Bangyan, without thinking, replied,' indicating an immediate response.
  • 即以倚翠對 - 'He immediately answered 'Yi Cui',' the name chosen.
  • 上惊喜 - 'The emperor was surprised and delighted,' a reaction phrase.
  • 謂與夢協 - 'Said it coincided with the dream,' indicating a connection between the dream and the name.
  • 時邦彦眷注已深 - 'At that time, Li Bangyan was already highly favored,' indicating political favor.
  • 有意大用 - 'Had the intention to use him greatly,' suggesting political promotion.
  • 自是數日間拜尚书右丞 - 'From that time, within a few days, he was appointed as the Right Chancellor of the Ministry of Works,' a high-ranking official post.
  • 遂為次相 - 'And thus became the second chancellor,' indicating promotion to a top ministerial position.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 宣和 - The Xuanhe era (1119–1125), the reign of Emperor Huizong of Song, known for his artistic patronage and the construction of Genyue (Gentle Mountain), a large imperial garden.

  • 徽宗 - Emperor Huizong of Song, a poet, artist, and patron of the arts, but also a controversial ruler whose reign led to the Jin-Song wars.

  • 唐杜甫詩 - A quote from Du Fu, one of the most revered poets of the Tang dynasty, known for his realism and emotional depth.
  • 天寒翠袖薄日暮倚修竹 - A line from Du Fu’s poem, likely from 'Qingpingyue' (Green Peace Ode) or a similar work, which evokes a woman in a green sleeve leaning on tall bamboo during dusk, symbolizing loneliness, elegance, and natural beauty.
  • 翰林學士 - A high-ranking literary official in the imperial court, part of the Hanlin Academy, responsible for drafting imperial edicts and advising the emperor.
  • 李邦彦 - Li Bangyan, a real historical figure, a Hanlin scholar and later a chancellor, known for his political opportunism and role in the fall of the Northern Song.
  • 尚书右丞 - The Right Chancellor of the Ministry of Works, a high-ranking administrative official in the Song bureaucracy.
  • 次相 - Second Chancellor, the second-highest official in the imperial government, second only to the Premier Chancellor.

  • 乃为置诸席间，而未之告也 - This phrase is elliptical, with '乃为置诸席间' meaning 'and placed it on the table,' and '而未之告也' meaning 'but did not tell him.' The sequence of events is implied.
  • 二千之入至微矣 - This phrase is elliptical, with '二千之入' meaning 'an income of two thousand coins,' and '至微矣' meaning 'is very small.' The contrast between the income and the dream is implied.

[906]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 所居去城十五里 - The place where he lived was fifteen li from the city; *去* means 'distance from,' and *所居* is a locative phrase meaning 'the place where one resides.'
  • 顷兵难时 - During the time of recent military calamity; *顷* means 'recently' or 'in the past,' and *兵难* refers to war or military disaster.
  • 多杀人于此 - Many people were killed here; *多* = 'many,' *杀人* = 'kill people,' *于此* = 'here.'
  • 一日、骑驴入城。过午而归 - One day, he rode a donkey into the city and returned after noon; *骑驴* = 'ride a donkey,' *入城* = 'enter the city,' *过午* = 'after noon.'
  • 经此桥 - Passing by this bridge; *经* = 'pass by' or 'go through,' *此桥* = 'this bridge.'
  • 见桥上下被发流血者 - He saw people with disheveled hair and flowing blood; *被发* = 'disheveled hair' or 'hair let down,' *流血* = 'flowing blood.'
  • 斩首断臂者 - People with severed heads and arms; *斩首* = 'severed head,' *断臂* = 'broken arms.'
  • 三两相扶 - Three or two people supporting each other; *三两* = 'three or two,' *相扶* = 'supporting each other.'
  • 莫知其极 - No one knew the extent; *莫知* = 'no one knows,' *其极* = 'the limit' or 'extent.'
  • 奇形异状 - Strange shapes and forms; *奇形* = 'strange shapes,' *异状* = 'unusual forms.'
  • 毫毛不能隐 - Not even a single hair could be hidden; *毫毛* = 'a single hair,' *不能隐* = 'could not be hidden.'
  • 惊而坠 - He was startled and fell; *惊* = 'startled,' *坠* = 'fell.'
  • 迨起。复见之如故态 - When he got up, he saw them again in their usual state; *迨* = 'until' or 'when,' *起* = 'rise,' *复见之* = 'saw them again,' *如故态* = 'as before.'
  • 且惊且走 - He was both startled and ran away; *且...且...* = 'both... and...'
  • 不敢开目 - He dared not open his eyes; *不敢* = 'dared not,' *开目* = 'open one’s eyes.'
  • 比至家 - When he reached home; *比* = 'when,' *至* = 'arrive,' *家* = 'home.'
  • 日已晡 - The sun had already set; *晡* = 'the time of the evening meal,' i.e., late afternoon or early evening.
  • 暮出舍前 - In the evening, he went out in front of his house; *暮* = 'evening,' *出* = 'go out,' *舍前* = 'in front of his house.'
  • 见田间水际亦如是 - He saw the same thing in the fields and by the water; *田间* = 'in the fields,' *水际* = 'by the water.'
  • 大怖而还 - He was greatly terrified and returned; *大怖* = 'greatly terrified,' *还* = 'return.'
  • 其归差早于前 - His return this time was earlier than before; *差早* = 'earlier,' *于前* = 'than before.'
  • 所见俨然 - What he saw was clear and present; *俨然* = 'clearly,' 'distinctly,' or 'as if present.'
  • 但正心澄念以待之 - But he tried to keep his heart upright and his thoughts clear in order to face it; *正心* = 'upright heart,' *澄念* = 'clear thoughts,' *以待之* = 'in order to face it.'
  • 悸魄稍定 - His terrified spirit was somewhat calmed; *悸魄* = 'terrified spirit,' *稍定* = 'somewhat calmed.'
  • 自是常有所睹 - From then on, he often saw them; *自是* = 'from then on,' *常* = 'often,' *有所睹* = 'saw something.'
  • 渐不加畏 - He gradually became less afraid; *渐* = 'gradually,' *不加畏* = 'not increasing in fear.'
  • 乡人颇知其事 - The villagers became somewhat aware of the matter; *颇知* = 'somewhat knew,' *其事* = 'the matter.'
  • 多往访焉 - Many went to visit him; *多* = 'many,' *往访* = 'went to visit,' *焉* = 'there.'
  • 韩总管丧爱子 - Han the Governor lost his beloved son; *总管* = 'Governor' or 'military commander,' *丧* = 'lost,' *爱子* = 'beloved son.'
  • 念之不忘 - He could not forget him; *念* = 'think of,' *不忘* = 'not forget.'
  • 召问沈 - He summoned Shen to ask him; *召* = 'summon,' *问* = 'ask,' *沈* = 'Shen' (a proper name).
  • 小人但见鬼物耳 - I only see ghosts and spirits; *小人* = 'I' (humble self-reference), *但* = 'only,' *见* = 'see,' *鬼物* = 'ghosts and spirits.'
  • 若追召遣逐不能也 - But I cannot summon, command, or drive them away; *若* = 'if,' *追召* = 'summon,' *遣逐* = 'send away.'
  • 吾正不为此 - I am not doing this for that reason; *正不为此* = 'not for that reason.'
  • 但恐儿魂魄尚幽滞 - But I fear that my son's spirit is still lingering in the other world; *幽滞* = 'lingering in the spirit world.'
  • 烦君一观之 - I ask you to look at it once; *烦* = 'bother,' *君* = 'you,' *观之* = 'look at it.'
  • 引诣昔所居 - He led him to the place where he had lived before; *引诣* = 'led to,' *昔所居* = 'the place where he used to live.'
  • 沈初不识 - Shen did not recognize it at first; *初不识* = 'did not recognize at first.'
  • 具言容貌举止所衣之服 - He described in detail the person's appearance, manner, and clothing; *具言* = 'describe in detail,' *容貌* = 'appearance,' *举止* = 'manner,' *所衣之服* = 'the clothing he wore.'
  • 与生时了不异 - Which was exactly the same as when he was alive; *了不异* = 'exactly the same.'
  • 立于室中 - Standing in the room; *立* = 'stand,' *于室中* = 'in the room.'
  • 韩举室大恸 - Han and his whole household wept greatly; *举室* = 'the whole household,' *大恸* = 'wept greatly.'
  • 其后问者不可以缕数 - After that, those who came to ask could not be counted one by one; *不可以缕数* = 'could not be counted one by one.'
  • 大抵皆如韩氏事 - Most of them were like Han's case; *大抵* = 'mostly,' *皆如* = 'were all like,' *韩氏事* = 'Han's case.'
  • 遂呼为沈见鬼 - They then called him 'Shen Who Sees Ghosts'; *遂呼为* = 'then called him,' *沈见鬼* = 'Shen Who Sees Ghosts.'

Syntactic Ambiguities

  • The sentence '乡人梦见之，说朋友间事甚详' is somewhat elliptical but can be understood as 'The villagers dreamt of him, and they spoke in great detail about matters among friends.'
  • The long sentence '时适谓豫乃济南人，既为御史矣，未知与同姓名者复何在，固不信也' can be broken down into a series of related clauses: 'Shi said that Liu Yu was from Jinan, he has already been appointed as a censor, he did not know where another person with the same name was, and he did not believe it at all.'
  • The sentence '乃中其两头不能伸缩，恶之，以与潜山观道士，使养于山间，不数日失去' describes a sequence of events: 'He passed the examinations, had two heads that could not extend or retract, hated it, gave it to a Taoist priest at Qianshan Temple, made him live in the mountains, and it was lost after a few days.'
  • The sentence '是冬、栋妻赵氏卒，以为不祥之兆，盖亦偶然耳' describes a series of events and a reaction: 'That winter, Dong's wife Zhao died, he considered it an ominous sign, and it was just a coincidence.'

  • The phrases '顶山寺缺', '萧六郎缺', '长生道人缺', '内文缺', '房州汤泉缺', '王君仪缺', '蜡屐亭诗缺' are elliptical and refer to missing or incomplete sections of the text. They should be rendered as 'missing content' or 'omitted passages'.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 守 - A title for a local governor or magistrate in ancient China.
  • 厢卒 - Guards or officers stationed at a gate or in a district, part of the local military or administrative structure.

  • 都邑 - The capital city, often a symbol of political power and prestige.
  • 发身 - A term for rising in status or position, often through merit or fortune.
  • 国门 - The gate to the capital city, symbolizing the threshold of power and authority.
  • 别除郡 - To be reassigned to a different prefecture or administrative region, often a sign of demotion or reassignment.
  • 逾年而卒 - To die after a year, often used to denote a relatively short time period in historical texts.
  • 不知所终 - A common expression in classical Chinese to indicate that a person’s fate or final destination is unknown or undocumented.

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 形貌狞恶 - A descriptive phrase meaning 'appearance fierce and evil,' used to describe the ghost’s looks.
  • 叱问之 - 'He shouted and asked them.' 叱 is a strong, commanding verb meaning 'to scold' or 'to shout at.'
  • 岁首之日 - 'On the first day of the year,' a synonym for 正旦.
  • 当行病于人间 - 'Should go and spread disease among people.' 行病 is a classical expression for 'to spread illness' or 'to cause disease.'
  • 吾家有之乎 - 'Is my family affected by this?' A rhetorical question.
  • 或三世积德 - 'Perhaps three generations of accumulated virtue.' 或 here is used in a list of possibilities.
  • 或门户将兴 - 'Or the family is about to rise in fortune.' 门户 refers to the family or household.
  • 或不食牛肉 - 'Or they do not eat beef.' This is one of the three possible reasons for being immune to the plague.
  • 三者有一焉 - 'If any one of these three is true.' 焉 is a particle used to conclude a list or a clause.
  • 遂不见 - 'So they disappeared.' 遂 means 'thus' or 'therefore,' and 不见 is 'did not appear.'

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 管枢密师仁 - The name 管师仁 (Guan Shirén) is a historical figure from the Song Dynasty, who held the title of Shu Mi (枢密), indicating he was a high-ranking military official.
  • 正旦 - The first day of the lunar New Year, a major festival in traditional Chinese culture, often associated with rituals and omens.
  • 疫鬼 - Plague spirits or disease spirits, a traditional belief in Chinese folklore that certain spirits cause illness or disaster at the beginning of the year.
  • 三世积德 - Refers to the traditional Confucian idea of moral accumulation over generations, where virtuous deeds by ancestors can benefit descendants.
  • 门户将兴 - A belief that a family's fortune or prosperity is on the rise, which can be a reason for divine protection or immunity from calamity.
  • 不食牛肉 - A dietary practice in some traditional Chinese sects or philosophies, possibly linked to Buddhist vegetarianism or Daoist taboos. In this context, it may be a symbolic or ritualistic practice rather than a strict rule.

Proper Nouns

  • 管枢密师仁 - The full name of the historical figure, with 管 as the surname, 师仁 as the given name, and 枢密 as a title or honorific.

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 登第出京 - Common phrase meaning 'after passing the imperial examination, he left the capital.' Refers to the traditional Confucian examination system and the journey of a newly successful scholar.
  • 憩道旁舍 - Literally 'rested at a roadside inn.' Refers to a common practice for travelers to stop at inns along the road.
  • 留飲之酒 - 'He was offered wine to drink.' A polite expression for being invited to drink, common in classical literature.
  • 望舍后横屋数楹 - 'Looking toward the back of the house, he saw several rooms.' The phrase 数楹 is an archaic way of saying 'several rooms' or 'several buildings.'
  • 甚少艾 - 'Very beautiful and youthful.' 少艾 is a classical expression meaning 'youthful and attractive.'
  • 注目不能去 - 'Could not look away.' A common expression used to describe being captivated or entranced.
  • 抵暮留宿 - 'Stayed overnight until evening.' This indicates the time of day when the guest decided to stay the night.
  • 主人夜与语 - 'The host spoke with him at night.' This suggests a more intimate or extended conversation.
  • 因及乡里门阀 - 'He then mentioned his hometown and family status.' 门阀 refers to family lineage or aristocratic status.
  • 审其未娶 - 'He found out he was unmarried.' 审 here means 'to ascertain' or 'to find out.'
  • 为言提举家一女 - 'He spoke of the daughter of the family of the tiju.' 提举 was an official title, often referring to a government office or official.
  • 极韶媚 - 'Extremely beautiful and charming.' 韶媚 is a classical term for beauty and grace.
  • 方相托议亲 - 'Who is now being proposed for marriage.' 议亲 refers to the process of arranging a marriage.
  • 子有意否 - 'Do you have any intention?' This is a direct and somewhat formal way of asking if the listener is interested.
  • 主人为平章 - 'The host mediated or arranged it.' 平章 can mean 'to mediate' or 'to settle matters.'
  • 翌日约定 - 'They made an agreement the next day.' This is a straightforward expression of making plans.
  • 女之母邀相见 - 'The mother of the girl invited him to meet.' This shows the mother's active role in the arrangement.
  • 钟爱息女 - 'She cherishes her daughter.' 息女 is a term for 'daughter,' used with affection.
  • 谋择对甚久 - 'They have been searching for a suitable match for a long time.' 择对 refers to choosing a suitable spouse.
  • 不意邂逅得佳婿 - 'Unexpectedly, they found a fine groom.' 邂逅 is a classical term for 'chance meeting' or 'unexpected encounter.'
  • 彼此在旅 - 'Both of us are traveling.' This suggests that the couple is in a temporary situation.
  • 不能具六礼 - 'We cannot perform the full six rites.' 六礼 refers to the traditional Chinese wedding rites.
  • 盍相与略之 - 'Why not just go through a simplified version?' 盍 is a classical particle meaning 'why not,' and 略之 means 'simplify it.'
  • 草草备聘财 - 'They hastily prepared the betrothal gifts.' 草草 means 'hastily' or 'carelessly.'
  • 择日成婚 - 'They chose a date to get married.' A straightforward expression of setting a wedding date.
  • 且许生挈女归济南 - 'And they allowed him to take the girl back to Jinan.' 挈 means 'to take along' or 'to bring.'

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 济南王生 - The title of the story. 王生 is a common name for a scholar or gentleman in classical Chinese stories. 济南 is a city in Shandong province, and the story likely takes place in that region.
  • 参政庆曾宗人也 - 'He was a relative of the cansheng Qing Zeng.' 参政 is a government title, meaning 'participating in government affairs' or 'counselor.' 宗人 means 'relative' or 'member of the same clan.'
  • 登第 - Refers to passing the imperial examination, a key step in the Confucian bureaucratic system.
  • 提举 - An official title, often used for local officials in charge of specific duties. It may also refer to a local government office or a family member who has a government position.
  • 六礼 - The traditional six rites of marriage in ancient China, including betrothal, engagement, wedding ceremony, etc.
  • 议亲 - The formal process of arranging a marriage, often involving the families of both parties.
  • 门阀 - A term referring to aristocratic or noble families, especially in the context of the gentry class during the Tang and Song dynasties.
  • 聘财 - The betrothal gifts or dowry given in a marriage arrangement.
  • 挈女归济南 - To take the bride back to the groom’s hometown, which was a common practice in traditional Chinese marriages.

  • 是夕仙乐闻于空浮之上 - '是夕' means 'that night,' '仙乐' means 'celestial music,' and '闻于空浮之上' means 'heard above the empty floating clouds.'
  • 留逾岁 - Means 'stayed for more than a year.'
  • 昼梦二天人与黄衣从者数百乘 - '昼梦' means 'daydream,' '二天人' means 'two celestial beings,' '黄衣从者' means 'yellow-robed attendants,' and '数百乘' means 'hundreds of carriages.'
  • 拥白虎来迎 - '拥' means 'accompanied by,' '白虎' means 'white tiger,' and '来迎' means 'to come and greet.'
  • 跨虎而行 - Means 'riding the tiger.'
  • 登危蹑险 - Means 'climbing dangerous and perilous paths.'
  • 由中峰入石洞 - Means 'entering the stone cave through the central peak.'
  • 顾视左右 - Means 'looking around.'
  • 金庭玉室 - Refers to a golden hall and jade chamber, symbolizing a heavenly abode.
  • 两青衣童入通 - '两青衣童' means 'two green-clad children,' and '入通' means 'to enter and report.'
  • 见茅君再拜谒 - '见茅君' means 'to see Master Mao,' and '再拜谒' means 'to pay respects twice.'
  • 君问劳甚厚 - '君' means 'the master,' '问劳' means 'to inquire about well-being,' and '甚厚' means 'very kindly.'
  • 帝已饬汝华阳洞天司命府丞 - '帝' means 'the Emperor,' '已饬' means 'has appointed,' '汝' means 'you,' '华阳洞天' means 'Huayang Celestial Abode,' '司命府丞' means 'Vice Minister of Life and Death.'
  • 因赐金尺以还 - '因' means 'therefore,' '赐' means 'bestowed,' '金尺' means 'golden ruler,' and '以还' means 'to return.'
  • 及寤 - Means 'upon waking.'
  • 别混康曰、吾数将尽 - '别' means 'to bid farewell,' '混康' is a proper name, '曰' means 'said,' and '吾数将尽' means 'my fate is coming to an end.'
  • 且有所授 - Means 'and something has been bestowed upon me.'
  • 从此逝矣 - Means 'I shall depart from here.'
  • 下投道人葛冲曰、敢以死累公 - '下投' means 'to descend and meet,' '道人' means 'Taoist priest,' '葛冲' is a proper name, '曰' means 'said,' '敢以死累公' means 'I dare to entrust my death to you.'
  • 预言八月十七日当解化 - '预言' means 'prophesied,' '八月十七日' means 'August 17th,' and '当解化' means 'will pass away.'
  • 及期、具衣冠端坐而卒 - '及期' means 'at the appointed time,' '具衣冠' means 'dressed in formal attire,' '端坐' means 'sitting upright,' and '而卒' means 'and died.'
  • 时建中靖国岁 - '时' means 'at that time,' '建中靖国' is a reign title, and '岁' means 'year.'
  • 春秋财六十一 - '春秋' means 'age,' '财' means 'about,' and '六十一' means 'sixty-one.'

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 真诰 - Refers to the *Zhenhao*, a Taoist text that records revelations from the celestial realm.
  • 华阳洞天 - Refers to the Huayang Celestial Abode, a mythical place in Taoist cosmology.
  • 上清箓 - Refers to the *Shangqing* talisman, a sacred object in Taoism.
  • 仙乐 - Refers to celestial music, a common motif in Taoist and Daoist literature.
  • 白虎 - In Taoist symbolism, the white tiger represents the west and is associated with metal and autumn.
  • 金庭玉室 - Refers to a heavenly abode, often used in Taoist texts to describe the dwelling of immortals.
  • 茅君 - Refers to Master Mao, a revered figure in Taoism, likely referring to one of the Eight Immortals.
  • 司命府丞 - Refers to the Vice Minister of Life and Death, a high-ranking official in the celestial bureaucracy.
  • 建中靖国 - Refers to a reign title of Emperor Huizong of Song, indicating the historical context of the text.

  • 刘混康 - Proper name of a Taoist priest.

  • 葛冲 - Proper name of a Taoist priest.
